$schema_note: Task-shaped checklists for recurring multi-file changes in this repo. Surfaced by .claude/hooks/workflow_hints.py (UserPromptSubmit) when the user's prompt matches a workflow's trigger phrases, so the checklist arrives BEFORE the first edit instead of being discovered file-by-file via the per-file hooks. Complements rayflow_file_map.json's 'systems' layer (which answers 'what does this file belong to'); this answers 'what does this KIND OF CHANGE usually touch'.

workflows.title: Adding a new node type
checklist: Implement the node class in rayflow/nodes/builtin/<file>.py (or a sandbox custom_nodes/ file for a one-off), decorated with @ray_node, @engine_node, or @parallel_node.
Give it an English `category`, consistent with existing ones (Math, Control, Loops, Events, ...) — don't introduce a new Spanish or inconsistent category.
Confirm rayflow/nodes/loader.py's auto-discovery picks it up (built-in dir is scanned automatically; no manual registration needed for built-ins).
Add coverage in tests/test_nodes.py.

If it introduces a genuinely new pattern (not just another instance of an existing one), consider whether rayflow/editor/guide.py's GUIDE text needs updating — it's what LLM agents read via get_guide/get_guide MCP tool.

workflows.title: Changing the SSE run-event schema (run_start/node_start/edge_fire/node_done/flow_done/flow_error)
checklist: Event emission lives in rayflow/engine/executor.py (FlowEngine) — all 5 events (node_start/edge_fire/node_done/flow_done/flow_error) are awaited on push now, not fire-and-forget: a driver draining the queue would otherwise see flow_done before per-node events still in flight, since only flow_done used to be awaited.
The consumer is rayflow/editor/frontend/src/hooks/useRunStream.ts — it drives the animation/state machine and has its own reconnect logic keyed on run_id.
Update the event table in CLAUDE.md if you add/rename/remove an event or its fields.
Check tests/test_executor.py and tests/test_editor.py for assertions on event shape/ordering.

The RunQueue actor (rayflow/state/queue.py) is a strict per-run FIFO once events reach its mailbox, but the actor's sequential event loop alone does NOT guarantee a driver sees them in order — that's exactly the bug that made all 5 pushes awaited (see above): a non-awaited push can still be in flight when a later awaited push (e.g. flow_done) already lands, so a driver draining the queue can see flow_done first and close the run early.

workflows.title: Adding a REST endpoint to the editor API
checklist: Add the route in rayflow/editor/routes.py (or custom_nodes_routes.py if it's about custom-node CRUD).

Decide whether it also needs an MCP tool — the MCP layer is a CURATED subset (rayflow/mcp/server.py), not auto-generated from the REST API, so exposing it to LLM agents is a deliberate extra step, not automatic.
Reuse existing storage/build/validator logic rather than duplicating it — both the REST route and any paired MCP tool should call the same underlying functions.
Add coverage in tests/test_editor.py (and tests/test_mcp.py if you added a tool).
Update the REST API table in CLAUDE.md.

workflows.title: Adding a new MCP tool
checklist: Add the tool function in rayflow/mcp/server.py with an English docstring — the docstring IS the tool description LLM clients see.
Call into existing editor/routes.py or storage.py logic rather than reimplementing it — the MCP layer is meant to reuse the REST API's logic, not duplicate it.
Error returns should be plain '{"error": "..."}' dicts in English, matching the existing 17 call sites' convention.
Add coverage in tests/test_mcp.py.
Update the MCP tool list in CLAUDE.md (the 'Capa MCP' section).

workflows.title: Changing the flow JSON schema (FlowDef/NodeDef shape)
checklist: Model changes go in rayflow/schema/models.py (plain stdlib @dataclass, not Pydantic — there's no pydantic dependency in this project) and rayflow/schema/loader.py (unknown-key warnings).
If the change affects validation/wiring semantics, rayflow/build/validator.py likely needs updating too (flatten/validate_all).
The frontend translator (rayflow/editor/frontend/src/lib/translator.ts) converts flowDef JSON <-> React Flow nodes/edges — check both directions still round-trip.

Update the flow JSON schema example in CLAUDE.md, README.md, and rayflow/claude_tools/skills/rayflow-flow/SKILL.md, and rayflow/editor/guide.py's GUIDE text — there's no packaged examples/ directory anymore (GET /editor/examples was removed), so these inline examples are the only reference.
Add coverage in tests/test_schema.py and tests/test_build.py.

workflows.title: Changing GraphState variable / OnVariableChange trigger semantics
checklist: Variable storage and watch_variable() live in rayflow/state/actor.py (GraphState) — set_variable only publishes to the event bus if the value actually changed.
Trigger wiring (var:{source}/{var} synthetic events) goes through rayflow/events/bus.py's EventBroker — matching is exact-string, including namespace.
The Set/Get node semantics live in rayflow/nodes/builtin/variables.py; the OnVariableChange entry point is in rayflow/nodes/builtin/events.py.
Frontend variable UI is rayflow/editor/frontend/src/components/VariablesPanel.tsx.
Watch for the known re-trigger-loop risk documented in CLAUDE.md if a flow watches and rewrites its own variable.
Add coverage in tests/test_events.py and tests/test_executor.py.

workflows.title: Adding or modifying a Claude Code hook in this repo
checklist: Shared logic goes in .claude/hooks/_file_map.py (or a new _*.py helper module) — hooks themselves should stay thin.
Register in .claude/settings.json under the right event; multiple entries with different matchers (or the same matcher) can coexist under one event.
Pipe-test with synthetic stdin before wiring it in, then prove it fires live (sentinel-prefix technique for Pre/PostToolUse; Stop/UserPromptSubmit/SessionStart can't be proven within the same turn).

If the hook is a new source file, add its entry to rayflow_file_map.json (staleness_guard.py will flag it as unmapped otherwise) — including its `system` (hooks-infra) so it shows up in the systems layer too.
Keep it silent-by-default and non-blocking unless the whole point is to block (like stale_reminder.py's decision:block) — hooks that error loudly on every tool call are worse than no hook.
